methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $3.5B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 8 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Space & Aerospace. Range: $1.0B - $4.2B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 45.3x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Apex Space
private.rationale: Same sector (Space & Aerospace), same stage (Series D), post-money $1B+

data.narrative:
Astranis, a Series D satellite communications company, carries a current post-money valuation of $3.5B following $350M+ in total raised capital. Valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of eight private-sector peers in Space & Aerospace at same or adjacent stages (Relativity Space, ICEYE, Axiom Space, Impulse Space, Varda Space Industries, and others) plus five publicly traded Space & Aerospace companies (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL).

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis, applying a typical 1.5x step-up multiplier from Series D to next-stage benchmarks, implies a valuation of $5.2B. Peer-median analysis across the eight comparable private companies yields a median valuation of $1.6B, with a range spanning $1.0B to $4.2B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied due to absence of target revenue data, a standard limitation with private companies. The five public comps trade at a median 45.3x price-to-sales, but without Astranis's disclosed revenue this approach remains impractical.

Triangulation of applicable methods supports a valuation range of $1.4B (low) to $6.0B (high), with a median of $3.5B. The current post-money valuation aligns precisely with the triangulated median. Key uncertainty centers on stage-progression timing and comparability weighting; peer valuations span a wide band relative to disclosed stage, and the absence of revenue or unit economics limits confidence in relative positioning within the peer set.
